title: Sersalisia chartacea
taxonomy: Sersalisia chartacea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Sersalisia, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs. The family Annonaceae is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple family. This family comprises over 2,000 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Sersalisia chartacea is native to Australia and is often found in the eastern and northern parts of the country.
uses: Sersalisia chartacea, also known as the brush cherry, has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its various health benefits.
